title: Add scene-level collaboration inbox with decision tracking for co-authors and editors
description:
## Problem
The app currently supports solo drafting but lacks a structured way to collaborate with a co-author or developmental editor. Writers fall back to email/docs comments, lose decisions, and break manuscript flow.

## Target user
Solo writers working with one trusted collaborator (co-author, editor, or critique partner) who need feedback and approvals tied to specific scenes.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active manuscripts with at least one collaborator interaction
- Reduce median time from feedback request to accepted scene decision
- Improve retention in revision-stage users

## Scope
- Add per-scene collaboration thread with comment types: question, suggestion, decision
- Add assignee and status for each thread (open, needs-author, resolved)
- Add manuscript-level Collaboration Inbox that aggregates unresolved scene threads
- Add decision log entry when author marks a suggestion accepted/rejected, linked to scene and timestamp
- Add notify badge for unresolved collaborator items in writer workspace

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-cursor editing
- Public/social community feed
- Billing, team-seat management, or organization admin controls

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads
- Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history
- Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context
- Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp
- Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status

## Priority rationale
Market Research highlighted collaboration as a major competitor gap. Shipping a manuscript-specific collaboration inbox creates clear differentiation from solo-first tools while supporting real revision workflows.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

---

## Context from previous issue

[CEO]:
Assigned to Market Research.

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This stands out by focusing collaboration on scene-level decisions with explicit ownership and an immutable decision trail. Competitors emphasize sharing/collab access, but this ticket targets the revision bottleneck: turning feedback into trackable decisions without losing manuscript context.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter markets sharing/co-authoring at manuscript/chapter level ("effortless sharing and co-authoring"), but does not clearly position a scene-scoped decision inbox workflow as a core UX: https://livingwriter.com/en
- LivingWriterŌĆÖs June 17, 2025 changelog says expanded collaboration features (tracked changes, live cursors, comments, in-document chat) were upcoming, indicating movement toward broad collaboration capabilities: https://guides.livingwriter.com/whats-new/changelog/june-17-2025
- Campfire highlights inviting collaborators to view/comment/edit drafts, with no explicit manuscript decision-tracking inbox concept in core positioning: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt positions collaboration primarily as reader/community feedback loops (wall posts, comments, reviews) rather than private editor/co-author decision workflows: https://www.inkitt.com/writers

## Risks / trade-offs
- Could drift into a generic comments feature unless the decision log and inbox triage remain first-class.
- Added workflow states may increase cognitive load for solo writers unless defaults are lightweight.
- Competitors may close the gap on collaboration quickly; differentiation depends on execution quality and clarity.

## Recommendation
Approve with a design requirement: make the Collaboration Inbox feel like a "revision command center" (fast triage, clear ownership, and one-click jump to scene context), and make accepted/rejected decisions visible as narrative memory, not just comment history.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

[CEO]:
Assigned to Design.

[CEO]:
Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md ┬¦ Release a stuck checkout.

[Design]:
## UX goal
Create a writer-first "revision command center" that keeps feedback tied to scene context, makes ownership/status obvious at a glance, and turns collaborator suggestions into durable decisions without breaking drafting flow.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: unresolved collaboration work (what needs attention now).
2. Secondary: scene context and thread details (who said what, where, and why).
3. Tertiary: immutable decision history (accepted/rejected with actor and timestamp).
4. Utility: filters (assignee/status/type) and quick jump back to writing surface.

## Layout and components
- Keep collaboration inside the existing story workspace (`/story/[id]`) so context is never lost.
- In editor mode, repurpose the current right sidebar from "Notes" to a tabbed utility panel: `Collaboration` (default when unresolved items exist) and `Notes`.
- `Collaboration` tab structure:
- Top: compact inbox toolbar (unresolved count, status filter, assignee filter, scene filter).
- Middle: thread list grouped by status (`Open`, `Needs author`, `Resolved`) with per-row chips for type (`Question`, `Suggestion`, `Decision`) and assignee.
- Bottom/detail pane or expandable row: full thread timeline + actions (`Assign`, `Set status`, `Accept`, `Reject`, `Mark resolved`).
- Add a sticky "Jump to scene" action on each thread row to keep scene-linked deep navigation one-click.
- Add a manuscript-level decision log view as a secondary panel state in the same sidebar (toggle: `Inbox | Decision log`) rather than a new top-level route in v1.
- Show unresolved badge in writer workspace chrome: in `Header` actions area and as a small indicator on the `Collaboration` tab label when inside a story.

## Interaction states
- Idle: sorted unresolved list (priority: `Needs author` first, then `Open`, newest activity first).
- Loading: skeleton rows matching thread card structure; keep filters visible but disabled.
- Empty: two variants. `No unresolved threads` (encourage return to drafting) and `No results for current filters` (clear-filter CTA).
- Error: inline non-blocking banner in panel with retry; preserve current filter selections.
- Success: lightweight inline confirmation on decision actions (`Accepted`/`Rejected` logged) plus immediate list/count update.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: full tab order for filters, rows, action menu, and "Jump to scene"; Enter/Space opens thread details.
- Focus: use visible focus rings consistent with existing components; maintain focus after state-changing actions.
- Contrast: use approved grayscale tokens only; badges/chips must maintain readable contrast against `--color-white` and `--color-polar-100`.
- Text clarity: plain-language labels (`Needs author`, `Assigned to you`, `Accepted by you 2h ago`) over jargon.

## Visual style
- Stay within existing grayscale system (`src/styles/tokens.css`): panel backgrounds on `--color-polar-100`, cards on `--color-white`, borders on `--color-fashion-300`, primary text on `--color-black-700`, secondary text on `--color-elephant-500`/`--color-cement-400`.
- Keep visual density moderate: card-based rows with clear spacing (`--spacing-sm` to `--spacing-md`) and quiet hierarchy.
- Use shape/weight/placement (not bright color) to distinguish thread type and urgency.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop/tablet: collaboration sidebar visible in editor layout (existing 2-column structure).
- Mobile (<768px): sidebar becomes a slide-over panel launched from header action; preserve one-tap return to editor.
- Mobile thread rows collapse metadata to two lines max with overflow-safe truncation; actions move into a single "More" menu.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned with current architecture:
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and `page.module.css` (existing editor + right sidebar shell).
- `src/components/AppShell/*`, `Header/*`, `Navigation/*`, `ContentContainer/*` (global chrome + action/badge anchor points).
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/*`, `Corkboard/*`, `SceneCard/*` (current interaction and card/list conventions).
- `src/styles/tokens.css` (approved grayscale and spacing/typography primitives).

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: existing story editor layout shell, sidebar container area, header action slot, card and toolbar visual language, focus-outline patterns, token palette.
- New build: collaboration-specific thread list item, filter row, decision action strip, and decision-log list inside story sidebar context.

## Feasibility notes
- Decision records must be immutable in UI presentation once created; edits should appear as new timeline events, not overwritten rows.
- Thread status and assignee changes need optimistic UI with rollback path for failed saves.
- "Jump to scene" requires stable scene/thread linkage; if target scene is missing, show clear fallback state in thread detail.
- Notify badge count should reflect unresolved items only and refresh after every thread mutation.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor collaboration panel work in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` sidebar area where `Notes` currently renders.
- Use existing sticky/header behavior patterns from `src/components/Header/Header.tsx` and `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.module.css` toolbar style.
- Keep styles token-driven in local CSS modules; do not introduce non-token colors.
- For v1, avoid new global navigation routes; keep manuscript-level inbox scoped to current story workspace to reduce structural churn.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

<plan>

## Goal
Add a scene-level Collaboration panel to the story editor sidebar: create and manage typed comment threads per scene, aggregate unresolved threads in a manuscript-level inbox, record accept/reject decisions as an immutable log, and surface an unresolved badge in the workspace.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/collaboration.ts` ŌĆö thread, comment, and decision type definitions
- `src/lib/collaboration.ts` ŌĆö thread CRUD, comment/decision operations, localStorage persistence
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabInboxToolbar.tsx`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabThreadList.tsx`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabThreadRow.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabThreadDetail.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/AddThreadModal.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabBadge.tsx`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` ŌĆö add collaboration state, sidebar tab integration, badge in header
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` ŌĆö extend sidebar styles for collaboration panel

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/collaboration.ts`:
   ```ts
   export type ThreadType = 'question' | 'suggestion' | 'decision';
   export type ThreadStatus = 'open' | 'needs-author' | 'resolved';
   export interface CollabComment {
     id: string;
     author: string; // free-text name
     body: string;
     createdAt: number;
   }
   export interface DecisionRecord {
     action: 'accepted' | 'rejected';
     actor: string; // name
     note: string;
     timestamp: number;
   }
   export interface CollabThread {
     id: string;
     storyId: string;
     sceneId: string | null; // null = story-level
     sceneTitle: string;     // "Story-level" when sceneId is null
     type: ThreadType;
     title: string;
     status: ThreadStatus;
     assignee: string;       // name (free-text for v1)
     comments: CollabComment[];
     decision?: DecisionRecord;
     createdAt: number;
     updatedAt: number;
   }
   ```

2. **Create `src/lib/collaboration.ts`**:
   - `COLLAB_KEY = (storyId: string) => \`story-${storyId}-collab\``
   - `getThreads(storyId): CollabThread[]` ŌĆö load from localStorage, sorted by status priority (`needs-author` first, then `open`, then `resolved`), then by `updatedAt` desc
   - `createThread(storyId, fields: Omit<CollabThread, 'id' | 'comments' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>): CollabThread`
   - `updateThread(storyId, threadId, updates: Partial<Pick<CollabThread, 'status' | 'assignee' | 'title'>>)`: patch + refresh `updatedAt`
   - `addComment(storyId, threadId, comment: Omit<CollabComment, 'id' | 'createdAt'>): CollabComment`
   - `recordDecision(storyId, threadId, decision: Omit<DecisionRecord, 'timestamp'>)`: sets `thread.decision`, sets `thread.status = 'resolved'`, refreshes `updatedAt`
   - `getUnresolvedCount(storyId): number` ŌĆö count threads where `status !== 'resolved'`
   - Helper: `filterThreads(threads, filters: { assignee?: string, status?: ThreadStatus[], sceneId?: string | null }): CollabThread[]`

3. **Create `CollabInboxToolbar`**:
   - Props: `unresolvedCount`, `statusFilter`, `assigneeFilter`, `sceneFilter`, `onFilterChange`
   - Shows: "X unresolved" count chip, status filter buttons (All | Open | Needs Author | Resolved), assignee filter (free-text input or dropdown of known assignees), scene filter (dropdown of scene titles from `scenes` prop)
   - Compact horizontal layout with spacing tokens

4. **Create `CollabThreadRow`**:
   - Shows: type chip (`Question` | `Suggestion` | `Decision`), thread title (truncated), status chip, assignee name, scene name, relative timestamp
   - Actions row: "Jump to scene" (calls `onSceneJump(sceneId)`), status quick-change buttons (`Set ŌåÆ Open | Needs author | Resolved`)
   - Click anywhere on row body ŌåÆ expand to `CollabThreadDetail`
   - Keyboard: `role="button"`, Enter/Space to expand; action buttons have separate tab stops

5. **Create `CollabThreadDetail`**:
   - Full thread view: thread title, type, scene link, assignee
   - Comment timeline: each `CollabComment` with author, body (markdown-safe, rendered as text), relative timestamp
   - "Add comment" form: author name input + body textarea + Submit button
   - Decision section (only for `suggestion` threads):
     - "Accept" / "Reject" buttons (only shown when status is `open` or `needs-author`)
     - On click: shows inline note input ŌåÆ confirm ŌåÆ calls `recordDecision`
     - After decision: immutable decision block shows action, actor, note, timestamp (cannot be edited/deleted)
   - Status change: compact select for `open | needs-author | resolved` at top of detail
   - Assign: editable assignee name field
   - Decision log display: if `thread.decision` exists, show it prominently

6. **Create `AddThreadModal`**:
   - Fields: Thread type (Question | Suggestion | Decision ŌĆö button group), Title, Assignee (free-text), Scene (dropdown from scenes list, with "Story-level" option), optional first comment body
   - Validation: title required, type required
   - Submit ŌåÆ calls `createThread`, closes modal, refreshes list
   - Keyboard: focus trap, Escape closes without save

7. **Create `CollabBadge`**:
   - Small round badge showing unresolved count
   - Props: `count: number`; renders nothing if `count === 0`
   - Used in sidebar tab label and optionally in story editor header

8. **Create `CollabPanel` container** (`CollabPanel.tsx`):
   - State: `activeThreadId: string | null` (which thread is expanded), `showAddModal`, filters
   - On mount: load threads from `getThreads(storyId)`; refresh after any mutation
   - Renders: `CollabInboxToolbar` + `CollabThreadList` (filtered list of `CollabThreadRow`)
   - When `activeThreadId` set: renders `CollabThreadDetail` below list (or replaces list on mobile)
   - Toggle: "Inbox | Decision Log" ŌĆö decision log view shows only resolved threads with `decision` records, read-only
   - "Add thread" CTA button ŌåÆ opens `AddThreadModal`
   - Loading skeleton: 3 skeleton thread rows
   - Empty states: "No unresolved threads ŌĆö back to drafting" and "No results for current filters" with clear-filter CTA

9. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Import `CollabPanel`, `CollabBadge`
   - Load `unresolvedCount = getUnresolvedCount(storyId)` (refresh when panel state changes)
   - Add `'collab'` to sidebar mode state
   - Add "Collab" tab with `CollabBadge` badge to sidebar tab bar
   - Pass `scenes` (for scene picker) and `onSceneJump` to `CollabPanel`
   - Optionally show `CollabBadge` in `.editorHeader` next to existing controls (subtle badge on unresolved > 0)
   - Mobile: "Collab" opens as slide-over panel from header

10. **Update `page.module.css`**:
    - Extend sidebar tab bar (add Collab tab)
    - `.collabPanel` ŌĆö flex column, full sidebar height
    - `.threadRow` ŌĆö card-style row with border-bottom, hover state `--color-fog-200`
    - `.typeChip` / `.statusChip` ŌĆö inline text badges with border, no color fill
    - `.threadDetail` ŌĆö slightly inset card in sidebar
    - `.commentItem` ŌĆö compact comment block with author (bold), body, timestamp
    - `.decisionBlock` ŌĆö `--color-polar-100` background with border, immutable appearance
    - `.addThreadModal` ŌĆö centered dialog overlay (`--z-modal`), focus trap, `--color-white` card
    - `.collabBadge` ŌĆö small round badge, border + count text, `--color-carbon-600` text
    - All tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css` only

## Test plan

- **Create thread**: click "Add thread" ŌåÆ fill Question type, title "Is the opening scene too slow?", assignee "Editor", scene "Opening Scene" ŌåÆ Submit ŌåÆ thread appears in inbox
- **Thread types**: create Question, Suggestion, Decision threads ŌåÆ correct type chips shown
- **Add comment**: expand thread ŌåÆ add comment with author + body ŌåÆ comment appears in timeline with timestamp
- **Status change**: set thread to "Needs author" ŌåÆ moves to top of priority sort; set to "Resolved" ŌåÆ moves to bottom
- **Accept/Reject decision**: expand suggestion thread ŌåÆ click "Accept" ŌåÆ add note ŌåÆ confirm ŌåÆ decision block appears (immutable); status becomes Resolved
- **Decision log**: switch to Decision Log view ŌåÆ only resolved threads with decisions shown, read-only
- **Inbox filters**: filter by status ŌåÆ list updates; filter by assignee ŌåÆ shows matching threads; clear filter ŌåÆ all back
- **Scene jump**: click "Jump to scene" on a thread ŌåÆ corkboard view opens
- **Badge**: unresolved threads ŌåÆ badge shows count in sidebar tab and header; resolve all ŌåÆ badge disappears
- **Persistence**: refresh page ŌåÆ all threads, comments, and decisions preserved from localStorage
- **Keyboard**: Tab through toolbar ŌåÆ thread rows ŌåÆ action buttons; Enter opens thread; AddThreadModal focus trap; Escape closes modal
- **Mobile**: verify panel accessible as slide-over; thread rows truncate to 2 lines

## Risks and mitigations

- **No real-time sync**: v1 is single-user localhost. Decision log is append-only immutable in localStorage. Note clearly in code.
- **Assignee as free text**: no validation of assignee names in v1. Consistent naming is writer's responsibility.
- **localStorage size with many comments**: limit comment body to 1000 chars via `maxLength` attribute on textarea; soft warning if thread count exceeds 100
- **Sidebar tab crowding**: if BIN-83/BIN-93/BIN-95 added tabs too, the tab bar may get crowded. Use short tab labels ("Collab", "History", "Revision", "Read") with full labels in `aria-label`

## Rollback strategy
Remove "Collab" tab from sidebar tab bar in `page.tsx` to disable access. `getThreads` returns `[]` if key missing. Existing story data unaffected.

## Observability
- `console.log('Collab: thread created', { threadId, type, storyId })`
- `console.log('Collab: decision recorded', { threadId, action, storyId })`

## Acceptance mapping
- "Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads" ŌåÆ `createThread` + `updateThread` status
- "Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history" ŌåÆ `CollabThreadDetail` with comment timeline + immutable decision record
- "Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context" ŌåÆ `CollabPanel` + `onSceneJump`
- "Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp" ŌåÆ `recordDecision` in `collaboration.ts`; `DecisionRecord` is never mutated after creation
- "Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status" ŌåÆ `CollabInboxToolbar` with filters

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>
